title: phragmites communis subsp. marsillianus
intro: Phragmites communis subsp. marsillianus, also known as Marsch reed, is a subspecies of the common reed plant (Phragmites australis) native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Phragmites communis subsp. marsillianus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared by making infusions, decoctions, or tinctures from its leaves and stems, which can be used to treat various health issues.
